cases.title: Position & Latitude
cases.description: Africa is the only continent crossed by these 3 major lines.
steps: The Equator (0°): Crosses Uganda, Kenya, DRC, Congo, Gabon, and Somalia.
Tropic of Cancer (23½°N): Passes through North Africa (Egypt, Libya, Algeria, etc.).
Tropic of Capricorn (23½°S): Passes through Southern Africa (Namibia, Botswana, S. Africa, Mozambique).
PLE Fact: Because of these lines, most of Africa lies in the Tropical Zone (Hot climate).

cases.title: The Four Corners of Africa
cases.description: The furthest points North, South, East, and West.
steps: North: Ras Ben Sakka (Tunisia).
South: Cape Agulhas (South Africa). Note: It is NOT Cape Town.
East: Cape Guardafui (Somalia). Located on the Horn of Africa.
West: Cape Verde Peninsula (Senegal).

cases.title: Oceans, Seas & Canals
cases.description: Africa is almost an island, separated from other continents by water.
steps: Strait of Gibraltar: Separates Africa (Morocco) from Europe (Spain).
Suez Canal: Man-made waterway separating Africa from Asia. Connects Red Sea to Mediterranean.
Strait of Bab-el-Mandeb: Separates Africa (Djibouti) from Asia (Yemen).
PLE Fact: The Suez Canal shortens the trade route to Europe.

cases.title: East African Community
steps: Members: Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi, South Sudan, DRC, and Somalia.
Ports: Uganda relies on Mombasa (Kenya) and Dar es Salaam (Tanzania) for overseas trade because it is landlocked.
Feature: Home to Lake Victoria (Largest fresh water lake in Africa).

cases.title: North & West Africa
steps: Nigeria (West): Most populated country in Africa. Major Oil producer (OPEC member).
Egypt (North): Gift of the Nile. Famous for the Aswan High Dam and Pyramids.
Libya (North): Major Oil producer. Large parts are Sahara Desert.
Ghana (West): Crossed by the Prime Meridian. Famous for Cocoa and Gold.

cases.title: Southern Africa
steps: South Africa: The most industrialized country in Africa.
Minerals: Famous for Gold and Diamond mining (The Rand).
History: Known for the struggle against Apartheid (Separation by race).
Environment: Home to the Kalahari and Namib deserts.

cases.title: Central Africa
cases.description: The heart of the continent, covered by the Congo Rainforest.
steps: Key Countries: DRC (Kinshasa), Congo (Brazzaville), Gabon, and Equatorial Guinea.
Forest: Home to the world's second largest rainforest.
River: The Congo River crosses the Equator twice.
